ccel.title: ANF04. Fathers of the Third Century: Tertullian, Part Fourth; Minucius Felix; Commodian; Origen, Parts First and Second

source.title: ANF04. Fathers of the Third Century: Tertullian, Part Fourth; Minucius Felix; Commodian; Origen, Parts First and Second
ANF04. Fathers of the Third Century: Tertullian, Part Fourth; Minucius Felix; Commodian; Origen, Parts First and Second

verses.text: Dost thou dissemble with the law that was given with such public announcement, crying out in the heavenly word of so many prophets?
If a prophet had only cried out to the clouds, the word of the Lord uttered by him would surely suffice.

The law of the Lord proclaims itself into so many volumes of prophets; none of them excuses wickedness; thus even thou wishest from the heart to see good things; thou art also seeking to live by deceits.
Why, then, has the law itself gone forth with so much pains?
Thou abusest the commands of the Lord, and yet thou callest thyself His son.
Thou art seen, if thou wilt be such without reason.
I say, the Almighty seeks the meek to be His sons, those who are upright with a good heart, those who are devoted to the divine law; but ye know already where He has plunged the wicked.
